mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month growth in retail sales signals improving household demand and near-term earnings momentum; higher RSAFS momentum predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the RSAFS 3-month momentum signal has Rank IC below 0.03 over two consecutive 3-year rolling windows or its top-quintile 20-day returns do not exceed bottom-quintile returns.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses below 0.25%, monetary policy is at an emergency-easing boundary that lowers discount rates and supports equity duration, producing positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in less than 50% of cases or average triggered returns are not above unconditional returns at p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment-rate crossing above 5.0% signals labor-market slack and recession-risk repricing; the resulting earnings-risk premium expansion predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if, after UNRATE crosses above 5.0%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered observations or the downside hit rate is below baseline.

mechanism_narrative: High housing starts combined with very low policy rates creates a credit-sensitive construction impulse that raises cyclical earnings expectations, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses above 1500 and FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0%
failure_condition: Reject if the joint HOUST-above-1500 and FEDFUNDS-below-1.0% state fails to deliver higher 60-day returns than either single condition alone over rolling 5-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices paired with falling industrial production indicates stagflation pressure that compresses margins and growth expectations, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth crosses above 3% and INDPRO 3-month growth crosses below 0%
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-CPI-growth and negative-INDPRO-growth state has nonnegative average 20-day returns or if the interaction term is not negative in walk-forward regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll gains accompanied by a falling unemployment rate identify a broad labor recovery that lifts aggregate income and revenue expectations, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month growth is positive and UNRATE 3-month change is below 0
failure_condition: Reject if the combined payroll-growth and falling-unemployment condition does not outperform unconditional 60-day S&P 500 returns by at least 50 basis points on average.

mechanism_narrative: A CPI acceleration while the funds rate is pinned near zero lowers ex ante real rates but raises inflation-scare risk; near-term valuation compression predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% and CPIAUCSL 1-month change crosses above 0.4%
failure_condition: Reject if 5-day forward returns after the joint trigger are positive in more than 55% of observations or if CPI acceleration has no incremental effect when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production crossing above its 2017-normalized level of 100 signals capacity utilization normalization and improving goods-sector profits, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO crossings above 100 are followed by negative average 5-day S&P 500 returns or if the post-crossing hit rate is not above the unconditional 5-day hit rate.
